trivia_bullets.text: Flavor text: "'You have a family. Mine died at Sea Gate. You go to yours, and I'll go to mine.'" [Scryfall]. No named speaker. Sea Gate was Zendikar's largest city — built atop a massive stone dam on Tazeem — and maintained the most organized resistance against the Eldrazi incursion [MTG Wiki: Sea Gate, via mtg.wiki]. The flavor text places this unnamed soldier as a survivor of Sea Gate's fall, fighting not for hope but for grief.

The oracle text — 'This creature attacks each combat if able unless you control another Ally' — mechanically encodes the flavor: the Cohort is reckless when alone, a solo survivor with nothing to hold back, but calms into unit discipline when fighting alongside fellow Allies. The self-attack drawback vanishes the moment another Ally is present, directly mirroring the flavor text's grief-without-community theme [Scryfall: oracle_text].

Artist: Volkan Baga [Scryfall] — the same artist as Ondu Champion (149) in this set. Pauper-legal, EDHREC rank 28831 [Scryfall: legalities, edhrec_rank] — deep bulk tier, but the flavor-text/mechanics alignment makes it a strong narrative object for bundling even at low market value ($0.04–$0.05) [Scryfall: prices].

In Battle for Zendikar, the Allies mechanic was reframed from the original Zendikar block's 'adventuring party' concept into a unified resistance army against the Eldrazi invasion. Reckless Cohort's type line — Human Warrior Ally — places it in that resistance force, though the flavor text reveals the soldier's motivation is personal loss rather than collective cause [WebSearch: BFZ Allies Rally mechanic; Scryfall: type_line].
sources: WebSearch: Battle for Zendikar Allies mechanic Rally keyword Zendikari resistance 2015
